fundamentals.name: Tower Semiconductor Ltd.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Tower Semiconductor Ltd., an independent semiconductor foundry, provides technology, development, and process platforms for integrated circuits in the United States, Japan, rest of Asia, and Europe. The company provides customizable process technologies, including SiGe, SiPho, mixed signal CMOS, RF CMOS, CMOS image sensor, non-imaging sensors, wafers, and integrated power management. It also offers a design enablement platform for a design cycle, as well as transfer optimization and development process services to integrated device manufacturers and fabless companies. The company serves consumer applications, personal computers, communications, handsets and smartphones, automotive, industrial, aerospace, and medical devices. Tower Semiconductor Ltd. was incorporated in 1993 and is headquartered in Migdal Haemek, Israel

judgment.summary: Tower Semiconductor shows strong growth and profitability metrics, but carries high valuation multiples and significant leverage.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 15.7% (EPS 0.65 vs 0.56); next earnings date is 2026-08-03.
positives: Revenue growth 15.5% YoY and earnings growth 62.9% YoY indicate robust expansion.
Profit margin 15.1% and operating margin 15.6% demonstrate solid profitability.
Forward P/E of 49.91x is lower than trailing P/E of 133.20x, suggesting expected earnings growth.
Strong analyst consensus of 'strong_buy' with a mean target of $313.83.
Significant silicon photonics contracts provide visibility into future revenue.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 133.20x and Price/Sales of 21.01x are very high, indicating a premium valuation.
PEG ratio of 8.17x suggests growth may not fully justify the current stock price.
Debt/Equity ratio of 5.24x indicates substantial financial leverage.
Return on equity of 8.7% is modest relative to the high valuation.

news.paragraph: Tower Semiconductor (TSEM) has secured significant growth drivers through substantial silicon photonics customer contracts, totaling $1.3 billion for 2027 revenue, with deals also anchoring data center and AI expansion. This news has propelled the stock upward, leading Wedbush to lift its price target and label Q1 commentary as positive, while other analysts are calling it a top high-return semiconductor stock. The company also reported a strong earnings surprise last quarter, with its next earnings date set for August 3rd.
